steps.title: Identify the mismatch
steps.content: The passage describes readers who “try to identify more and more real-life analogues” in a novel that “is largely pure invention.” This contradiction—searching for real-world basis in mostly fictional material—creates the risk.

steps.title: Apply the logic to the choices
steps.content: When you search for real-world connections in a mostly fictional work, you propose links without actual factual support—the unsupportable connections choice A describes. The other choices either reverse what the passage says readers are doing, introduce unrelated topics, or miss the problem entirely. That matches choice A — A is correct.
